evidence: Re-verified after the v4.4.18 fix (ad11a35). Real posttool-recorder hook executable + documented payload {exit_code:0, interrupted:false}: recorded exit_code=0 (was -1), test_command_seen=True, session_test_run_seen=True (was permanently False). Root cause confirmed empirically: 14,254 real Bash rows on this installation all recorded -1 under the old returnCode key; the only 0 was a synthetic probe hand-fed that key.
correctness: Now correct: the signal can be satisfied by a genuine passing run, and cannot be satisfied by a failing (exit 1) or interrupted run. Previously it asserted 'no recorded passing test run' when passing runs had occurred.
effectiveness: Now effective. Before the fix it was unsatisfiable and nagged every source-touching turn forever, training the user to ignore it. LIMITATION: end-to-end confirmation that the nudge goes silent needs a session started on 4.4.18; this session is pinned to 4.4.15, so the final link is verified at the recorder/consumer boundary, not by observing the live nudge.
